key_insight: Physics-Informed Broad Learning System (PIBLS) turns PDE solving into a direct least-squares problem, enabling a backpropagation-free, provably universal approximator for PDEs.
engineering_relevance: PIBLS offers a fast, mesh-free alternative to PINNs for scientific computing, making real-time simulation and design optimization more practical.
method: Use a broad learning architecture with physics-informed constraints to solve PDEs.
Formulate training as a direct least-squares optimization instead of gradient-based backpropagation.
Extend the framework with an improved algorithm for nonlinear PDEs and prove universal approximation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experiments on linear and nonlinear PDEs where PIBLS is 1–3 orders of magnitude faster than conventional PINNs and achieves higher accuracy.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and stability of the least-squares solve, so large or ill-conditioned PDE systems may require careful numerical implementation.
paper_title: Learning universal approximations for partial differential equations with Physics-Informed Broad Learning System
